methods.name: does_account_exist
methods.signature: xrpl.account.does_account_exist(address: str, client: SyncClient, ledger_index: str | int = 'validated') → bool
methods.description:
Query the ledger for whether the account exists.
address – the account to query.
client – the network client used to make network calls.
ledger_index – The ledger index to use for the request. Must be an integer ledger value or “current” (the current working version), “closed” (for the closed-and-proposed version), or “validated” (the most recent version validated by consensus). The default is “validated”.
Whether the account exists on the ledger.
XRPLRequestFailureException – if the transaction fails.
parameters: client – the network client used to make network calls.

ledger_index – The ledger index to use for the request. Must be an integer ledger value or “current” (the current working version), “closed” (for the closed-and-proposed version), or “validated” (the most recent version validated by consensus). The default is “validated”.
returns: Whether the account exists on the ledger.

methods.name: get_account_root
methods.signature: xrpl.account.get_account_root(address: str, client: SyncClient, ledger_index: str | int = 'validated') → Dict[str, int | str]
methods.description:
Query the ledger for the AccountRoot object associated with a given address.
address – the account to query.
client – the network client used to make network calls.
ledger_index – The ledger index to use for the request. Must be an integer ledger value or “current” (the current working version), “closed” (for the closed-and-proposed version), or “validated” (the most recent version validated by consensus). The default is “validated”.
The AccountRoot dictionary for the address.
parameters: client – the network client used to make network calls.

ledger_index – The ledger index to use for the request. Must be an integer ledger value or “current” (the current working version), “closed” (for the closed-and-proposed version), or “validated” (the most recent version validated by consensus). The default is “validated”.
returns: The AccountRoot dictionary for the address.

methods.name: get_balance
methods.signature: xrpl.account.get_balance(address: str, client: SyncClient, ledger_index: str | int = 'validated') → int
methods.description:
Query the ledger for the balance of the given account.
address – the account to query.
client – the network client used to make network calls.
ledger_index – The ledger index to use for the request. Must be an integer ledger value or “current” (the current working version), “closed” (for the closed-and-proposed version), or “validated” (the most recent version validated by consensus). The default is “validated”.
The balance of the address.
parameters: client – the network client used to make network calls.

ledger_index – The ledger index to use for the request. Must be an integer ledger value or “current” (the current working version), “closed” (for the closed-and-proposed version), or “validated” (the most recent version validated by consensus). The default is “validated”.

methods.name: get_latest_transaction
methods.description:
Fetches the most recent transaction on the ledger associated with an account.
account – the account to query.
client – the network client used to communicate with a rippled node.
The Response object containing the transaction info.
XRPLRequestFailureException – if the transaction fails.
parameters: client – the network client used to communicate with a rippled node.
returns: The Response object containing the transaction info.

methods.name: get_next_valid_seq_number
methods.signature: xrpl.account.get_next_valid_seq_number(address: str, client: SyncClient, ledger_index: str | int = 'current') → int
methods.description:
Query the ledger for the next available sequence number for an account.
address – the account to query.
client – the network client used to make network calls.
ledger_index – The ledger index to use for the request. Must be an integer ledger value or “current” (the current working version), “closed” (for the closed-and-proposed version), or “validated” (the most recent version validated by consensus). The default is “current”.
The next valid sequence number for the address.
parameters: client – the network client used to make network calls.

ledger_index – The ledger index to use for the request. Must be an integer ledger value or “current” (the current working version), “closed” (for the closed-and-proposed version), or “validated” (the most recent version validated by consensus). The default is “current”.
returns: The next valid sequence number for the address.
